subject: Anthropology
title: Pidgins and Creoles
description: Learn Pidgins and Creoles in one minute.

hook.text: Two groups with no shared language meet to trade. They invent a simple hybrid tongue. A pidgin is born.

buildup.text: Pidgins are simplified contact languages created when groups without a common language need to communicate.

discovery.text: When children grow up speaking a pidgin natively, it becomes a creole — with full grammar and complexity.

twist.text: Creolization takes one generation. Children naturally add grammar that the pidgin lacked. Language wants structure.

climax.text: Pidgins and creoles show that humans are born to build language. Give them contact and they create full communication.

punchline.text: Need creates language; children perfect it.

quiz.question: What turns a pidgin into a creole?
options: Children learning the pidgin as their native language
